spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates autocorrect='on' vs 'off' on contenteditable authoring surfaces and reads the IDL property live.

interactive.rationale: Real controls switch the right-pane scenario, insert samples, sync text, clear panes, and exercise undo/event logging.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index blurb matches the two-editor content-authoring workflow and live state table.

edge_cases.rationale: Adds a controlled event/undo probe that logs beforeinput/input, inputType, insertReplacementText when emitted by OS autocorrect, and historyUndo when undo is exposed.

references.rationale: Includes a see-also section with the canonical chromestatus.com/feature/6264645053710336 link.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout; no raw hex colours remain in the concept HTML.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: Two contenteditable surfaces (prose vs precision), a switchable right pane, live attribute state, and an event/undo probe that shows autocorrect replacement and history undo inputTypes when the platform emits them. The page embeds the canonical ChromeStatus link. Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp: scenario switches, undo/event rows, fallback IDL readout, and clean console/network all worked in the tested Canary.
